ticket.description:
Hi there,

How to fix the Calculated transit time shown to customers using automated rates? For example it is wrongfully showing U.S. customers 1-2 business days which is impossible.

Thank you

------------------
Submitted from: https://mcsl-s.pluginhive.io/home/settings/carriers/dhlexpress/6ba229ed-ca62-4d71-a96a-b0785c067261

comments.body:
Hello Matt,

Good day!

The transit time shown at checkout for automated rates comes directly from the DHL Express API, calculated based on your configured shipper origin address and the customer's destination.

You can replicate a transaction on the DHL portal and check for the delivery days provided there.

For more details, we request you to check with the carrier team.

Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Matt,
 
Good day!

The transit time shown at checkout for automated rates comes directly from the DHL Express API, calculated based on your configured shipper origin address and the customer's destination.
 
You can replicate a transaction on the DHL portal and check for the delivery days provided there. 
 
For more details, we request you to check with the carrier team.
 
Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Sagar,


Thank you for the quick response.



DHL shows 2 total transit days never 1, where does does the 1 in 1-2 days come from?



Could we please:

1. disable it?

2. edit or add additional days ontop?



Thank you
comments.html_body:
Hi Sagar,

Thank you for the quick response.

DHL shows 2 total transit days never 1, where does does the 1 in 1-2 days come from?

Could we please:

2. edit or add additional days ontop?

Thank you
comments.plain_body:
Hi Sagar, 
  Thank you for the quick response.  
  DHL shows 2 total transit days never 1, where does does the 1 in 1-2 days come from?  
  Could we please:  1. disable it?  2. edit or add additional days ontop?  
  Thank you

comments.body:
Hello Matt,

Thank you for your response.

I understand that DHL is providing two transit days and the same will be passed to shopify through the app. The shopify consider only the business days and show the delivery days at the checkout.

1. To disable this, Navigate to App Settings -> General Settings -> Shipping -> Disable option "Estimated Delivery At Checkout" -> Save (as shown below)

2. Currently, there is no option to provide additional days to the delivery days provided by carrier. Will check with the team for the possibilities and get back to you


Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Matt,
 
Thank you for your response. 
 
I understand that DHL is providing two transit days and the same will be passed to shopify through the app. The shopify consider only the business days and show the delivery days at the checkout.
 
  1. To disable this, Navigate to App Settings -> General Settings -> Shipping -> Disable option "Estimated Delivery At Checkout" -> Save (as shown below)
     
  2. Currently, there is no option to provide additional days to the delivery days provided by carrier. Will check with the team for the possibilities and get back to you
     
Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Sagar,


We reviewed many shipments in DHL and they previously say 2 or 3 or 4 days.



For these U.S. shipments they only ever said 2 at the minimum, never 1, so why did the auto rates show "1-2 days"?



Also, DHL provides "Total transit days" and "Est. delivery date", can you use "Est. delivery date" instead?

> ![screenshot.png](https://support.pluginhive.com/attachments/token/cMkjVXDpIdcuvazURpgjJe8H6/?name=screenshot.png)



DHL replied to our enquiry "the portal calculates transit times and estimated delivery dates using a combination of operational factors. The total transit days are typically based on standard transit time ranges between the selected origin and destination, under normal conditions."



Thanks
comments.html_body:
Hi Sagar,

We reviewed many shipments in DHL and they previously say 2 or 3 or 4 days.

For these U.S. shipments they only ever said 2 at the minimum, never 1, so why did the auto rates show "1-2 days"?

Also, DHL provides "Total transit days" and "Est. delivery date", can you use "Est. delivery date" instead?

DHL replied to our enquiry "the portal calculates transit times and estimated delivery dates using a combination of operational factors. The total transit days are typically based on standard transit time ranges between the selected origin and destination, under normal conditions."

Thanks
comments.plain_body:
Hi Sagar, 
  We reviewed many shipments in DHL and they previously say 2 or 3 or 4 days.  
  For these U.S. shipments they only ever said 2 at the minimum, never 1, so why did the auto rates show "1-2 days"?  
  Also, DHL provides "Total transit days" and "Est. delivery date", can you use "Est. delivery date" instead?        
  DHL replied to our enquiry "the portal calculates transit times and estimated delivery dates using a combination of operational factors. The total transit days are typically based on standard transit time ranges between the selected origin and destination, under normal conditions."  
  Thanks

comments.body:
Hello Matt,

Good day!

I have checked a recent transaction details under the App Settings -> Shipping Rates -> Request Log (as shown in below screenshot) and estimated delivery days provided for this transaction by DHL is 4 days.

As conveyed in the previous response, we pass the same number of days to the shopify along with the delivery date and the shopify will consider only the business days and show the final delivery days on the checkout. Also, showing the delivery date on the checkout is in the control of shopify and the app does not have control over it.

 ![](https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/attachments/token/4SvJhYVVqF4C3KiCTz7VYUHYF/?name=image.png)

Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Matt,
 
Good day!

I have checked a recent transaction details under the App Settings -> Shipping Rates -> Request Log (as shown in below screenshot) and estimated delivery days provided for this transaction by DHL is 4 days. 
 
As conveyed in the previous response, we pass the same number of days to the shopify along with the delivery date and the shopify will consider only the business days and show the final delivery days on the checkout. Also, showing the delivery date on the checkout is in the control of shopify and the app does not have control over it. 
 

 
Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Matt,

Thank you for sharing the details.

The estimated delivery days are shared by the app to Shopify to show on checkout. Shopify usually updates the estimate and adds the additional data on checkout.
If this is creating any difficulties in your shipping process, please share more information about the same and we will check the case further.

Additionally, could you also please confirm if the Estimated Delivery Dates option in Shopify Settings > Shipping and Delivery is enabled on your store as in sample screenshot below?

If it is enabled, could you please disable it and try checking for the rates and estimates on checkout again?

Let us know how it goes.

 ![](https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/attachments/token/Hc3oPDLeWSYt0G6jJXU5f5PpJ/?name=image.png)

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Matt,
 
Thank you for sharing the details.
 
The estimated delivery days are shared by the app to Shopify to show on checkout. Shopify usually updates the estimate and adds the additional data on checkout.
If this is creating any difficulties in your shipping process, please share more information about the same and we will check the case further.
 
Additionally, could you also please confirm if the Estimated Delivery Dates option in Shopify Settings > Shipping and Delivery is enabled on your store as in sample screenshot below?
 
If it is enabled, could you please disable it and try checking for the rates and estimates on checkout again?
 
Let us know how it goes.
 

 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Touseef,


This is already disabled.



The problem is the app is showing "1 to 2 business days" when DHL only shows 2 days, never one.



Where does the "1" come from?
comments.html_body:
Hi Touseef,

This is already disabled.

The problem is the app is showing "1 to 2 business days" when DHL only shows 2 days, never one.

comments.plain_body:

comments.body:
Hello Matt,

Thank you for your response and confirmation.

The estimated delivery display format is decided by Shopify to show on checkout. The app is passing the exact delivery date to Shopify for the service and Shopify is using its standard format to show the estimated delivery on checkout.
So, the "**1 to**" in "1 to 2 Business Days" is being included from Shopify on checkout.

You can also try creating a flat rate in Shopify and assign estimated delivery to the rate and check the case.

Let us know if you have any further queries.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Matt,
 
Thank you for your response and confirmation.
 
The estimated delivery display format is decided by Shopify to show on checkout. The app is passing the exact delivery date to Shopify for the service and Shopify is using its standard format to show the estimated delivery on checkout.
So, the "1 to" in "1 to 2 Business Days" is being included from Shopify on checkout.
 
You can also try creating a flat rate in Shopify and assign estimated delivery to the rate and check the case.
 
Let us know if you have any further queries.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
